results.title: 5 Leading Commodities Mutual Funds in India - Views on News from Equitymaster
excerpts:
Section Title: 5 Leading Commodities Mutual Funds in India > \ SBI Gold Fund > \ Tata Resources & Energy Fund
Content:
[Tata Resources and Energy Fund](https://www.personalfn.com/mutual-funds/best-funds/factsheet/tata-resources-energy-fund-reg-g?utm_source=the-fundstrategist&utm_medium=website&utm_campaign=e-letter&utm_content=the-fundstrategist) provides exposure to a diverse basket of commodities and energy sectors.
The fund invests about 80% of its AUM in the commodities, utilities, and energy sectors.
Also, the fund manager maintains a balanced portfolio of growth and value stocks.
That is, the fund comprises a mix of secular (non-cyclical) businesses that can deliver growth even during a downturn and cyclical plays that can outperform when specific themes take off (e.g., metals, cement).
To provide downside protection, the scheme may also allocate a portion of AUM to debt and money market instruments.
Section Title: 5 Leading Commodities Mutual Funds in India > \ SBI Gold Fund > \ Tata Resources & Energy Fund
Content:
Fund managers select stocks after rigorous fundamental research into factors such as management, business competitiveness, corporate governance, growth prospects, and past performance.
The fund currently has about 98.46% of its portfolio invested in equities, with the remaining 1.54% is held as cash and cash equivalents.
The portfolio adopts a balanced market-cap mix, with 54.17% in large caps for stability, while midcap (18.7%) and small-cap (27.13%) exposure adds higher growth from commodity upcycles.
As of 31 December 2025, the fund held 23.19% of AUM in metals and mining, positioning it to benefit from the sharp January surge.
Oil, gas, and consumable fuels accounted for 19% of the portfolio, providing additional exposure to energy-linked price cycles.
Section Title: 5 Leading Commodities Mutual Funds in India > \ SBI Gold Fund > \ Tata Resources & Energy Fund
Content:
UltraTech had the highest weighting at 6.75%, providing stability and exposure to the infrastructure sector. Vedanta had a 6.68% weight, providing exposure to zinc, copper, silver, and aluminum, whose prices have risen significantly recently.
Other top holdings include Ambuja Cement (5.01%), Tata Steel (4.42%), and Reliance Industries (3.86%).
 ... 
Section Title: 5 Leading Commodities Mutual Funds in India > \ SBI Gold Fund > \ Kotak Nifty Commodities Index Fund
Content:
[Kotak Nifty Commodities Index Fund](https://www.personalfn.com/mutual-funds/best-funds/factsheet/kotak-nifty-commodities-index-fund-reg-g?utm_source=the-fundstrategist&utm_medium=website&utm_campaign=e-letter&utm_content=the-fundstrategist) tracks the Nifty Commodities Index.
The Nifty Commodities Index is a sectoral index that tracks the performance of companies that derive a significant portion of their revenue from commodity-related businesses.
It includes stocks from segments such as metals, oil and gas, chemicals, cement, and fertilisers, offering a consolidated view of how commodity-linked sectors perform in the Indian equity market.
As of 31 December, power accounted for 20.08% of the asset allocation, followed by petroleum products (18.73%), cement and cement products (15.21%), ferrous metals (14.28%), and non-ferrous metals (6.15%).
Section Title: 5 Leading Commodities Mutual Funds in India > \ SBI Gold Fund > \ Kotak Nifty Commodities Index Fund
Content:
This offers investors diversified exposure across energy, metals, and core industrial sectors, reducing reliance on a single commodity cycle while staying aligned with broader economic demand trends.
The fund is tilted towards largecap which accounted for 80.80% of the portfolio, followed by mid-cap stocks (19.20%).
This positioning is mirrored in stock selection. Reliance Industries accounted for 9.88% of the portfolio, offering exposure across the oil-to-chemicals value chain. This is followed by NTPC at 7.49%, reflecting the theme of rising energy demand.
Tata Steel stood at 7.14%, while UltraTech Cement (6.17%) and Hindalco (6.15%) providing exposure to materials and construction-linked demand.

Section Title: ... > ### About Tata Resources & Energy Fund Direct Growth
Content:
Tata Resources & Energy Fund Direct Growth is a Equity Mutual Fund Scheme launched by Tata Mutual Fund. This scheme was made available to investors on 30 Jun 1995. Satish Chandra Mishra is the Current Fund Manager of Tata Resources & Energy Fund Direct Growth fund. The fund currently has an Asset Under Management(AUM) of ₹2,00,515 Cr and the Latest NAV as of 10 Apr 2026 is ₹55.29.
The Tata Resources & Energy Fund Direct Growth is rated Very High risk. Minimum SIP Investment is set to ₹100. Minimum Lumpsum Investment is ₹5,000. Exit load of 0.25% if redeemed within 30 days.
;
Section Title: Tata Resources & Energy Fund Direct Growth > ... > Investment Objective
Content:
The scheme seeks long term capital appreciation by investing atleast 80% of it's net assets in equity/equity related instruments of the companies in the Resources & Energy sectors in India.
Fund benchmark NIFTY Commodities Total Return Index
[Scheme Information Document(SID)](https://www.tatamutualfund.com)
